arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with nowsecure/frida-scripts (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with akabe1/frida-multiple-unpinning (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with AloneMonkey/frida-ios-dump (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Dynamic instrumentation toolkit (Python + JS) category. Fits when the engagement needs: Dynamic instrumentation toolkit for developers, reverse-engineers, and security researchers. Learn more at frida.re.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Dynamic instrumentation toolkit for developers, reverse-engineers, and security researchers. Learn more at frida.re.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install frida-tools # CLI tools; pip install frida       # Python bindings. Dynamic instrumentation toolkit for developers, reverse-engineers, and security researchers. Learn more at frida.re.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases, and licence before use. Operates against the operator's own authorized scope only.

use_cases: When you need Dynamic instrumentation toolkit (Python + JS), use frida because Dynamic instrumentation toolkit for developers, reverse-engineers, and security researchers. Learn more at frida.re.
When chaining Dynamic instrumentation toolkit (Python + JS) tools, pair frida with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `frida --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
